mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market acts as a leading indicator of imminent corporate revenue contraction, causing equity markets to price in lower earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal, or the signal shows no predictive power for earnings revisions.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of accelerating consumer price inflation and a tightening monetary stance compresses real disposable income while raising borrowing costs, simultaneously crushing demand and equity multiples to drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression but SPX forward returns remain positive, or the joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production signals robust manufacturing output and corporate capacity utilization, which directly supports earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns in high INDPRO growth periods do not exceed the median return of the full sample, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth reflects expanding aggregate demand, which historically correlates with rising corporate revenues and earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between GDP growth surprises and SPX forward returns falls below 0.10 for three consecutive years, indicating a structural decoupling of macro growth from equity pricing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts serves as a high-frequency leading indicator of weakening consumer confidence and construction sector distress, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when HOUST drops below the threshold, or the signal generates more false positives than true negatives in a 12-month walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of strong non-farm payroll growth and a persistently low unemployment rate indicates a labor market that is both expanding and fully utilized, which historically fuels wage-driven inflation and forces aggressive central bank tightening, ultimately depressing equity valuations and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to predict a decline in equity valuations, or periods of strong payrolls combined with low unemployment consistently produce positive SPX forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rapid month-over-month increases in the consumer price index signal eroding real purchasing power and rising input costs, which compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL accelerates above the regime threshold, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.
